channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET? — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-04-26 17:16 UTC

OP

Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62158601
>>62158557
Neet
Wagie can get some money to go out and have fun, and socialize.
might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

62158623
>>62158601
>might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

while waging at walmart is impossible

62158626
>>62158557
I prefer to be a neet, but ofc neither situation is perfect. Now if i was getting pussy then we'd be talking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62158627
>>62158601
>everything for pussy
Why subhuman ape nigger life always revolves around pussy and femoids?

62158639
>>62158627
probably a stupid dalit

62158643
>>62158557
I'd rather be a wagie at a comfy job but if it's somewhere like Walmart I'd rather be a neet

62158662
>>62158639
Checked, you know they are subhuman browns once they talk about their fantasies about femoids

62158666
>>62158627
>>62158623
>>62158627
>>62158639
Dude it’s not about pussy. I said it’s a great motivation when it happens to you.
It’s just one example.
How about playing ck3, or total war, or stellaris and just having a blast? Guess what? You need a great pc and money
Everything is fucking great when you have money, it’s the freedom, it’s the options.
I thought being neet is good, I was happy with “existing”
Playing my free pirated games on my shit laptop and “existed”
Couldn’t even get out because “everything is expensive”
With a job, a shit one at that, I paid for a laptop.
With only $40 I bought all warhammer total war series with most of dlc and with mods I can play that shit for life
Now I can go out, and dance, and go buy a some outfit from thriftshops
I am still making shit, but now I can LIVE, I can get motivated because I tasted a little of good life on my mouth.
Now I don’t wanna go back to pirate shitty games and use a shit laptop becaus that sucked, now I don’t wanna stay inside all weekends because I know how fun it can be.
It’s about change in frame of reference, because you never know how bad that shit is until you change your life.
Former neet

62158667
NEET.

62158671
>>62158662
Checked what you transplant. What is this? Is it summer already?
However, you get a checked

62158682
>>62158666
>>62158671
>I bought all warhammer total war series with most of dlc and with mods I can play that shit for life
Holy jeet shit spawn

62158709
>>62158682
Fucking nigger cattle transplant. You don’t even know what fucking “checked” means
Who you even schooling boi? Learn the lingo and try to fit in

62158721
>>62158682
In all honestly, maybe I am just build different.
I find motivation in the little things and I let them propel me forward.
Also I don’t have any social media so I am immune to algorithms corrupting my world view with endless doom and gloom about women and money

62158724
>>62158709
You lost jeet tranny

62158726
>>62158557
wagie is more soul crushing, but neet is lower quality of life. Was a neet for about 3 years and it was nice but I had to be very specific and intentional with how I spent money and how often I hungout with friends. Now I have a wagie job and even though I'm not making the big bucks I don't have to triple check my banking app when I go out to dinner with my friends which is such a nice feeling. If you can find a job you (sometimes you don't even need to like it if you at least like your coworkers a lot) then waging is better.

62158741
>>62158724
No please mr transplant. Throw the “checked” around a few more times, it might stick this time.
Fucking embarrassing
>>62158726
Exactly.
But you know what I realized? If you are slider who never does shit in his life when he has a job (go out, try new shit) wouldn’t do anything with his free time either, when he is a neet.
A lot of people are like that, they keep complaining about their jobs, unable to separate regular hours from working hours

62158746
>>62158741
>mr
Saaaaar

62158752
>>62158746
>help help I can’t 4chan lingo
>I keep calling everyone “checked”
pack it up jamboi

62158759
>>62158752
>saaar 4chin
>I need vpn to post on 6 different IDs saaar
Saaaar let me guess next post will have a different ID?

62158776
Most women are NEETs

62158784
>>62158759
dude you are just a transplant, when you can’t even realize the difference between a pc poster vs a phone poster (hence Id shifting)
How many more fucking Ls can you fit into your ass faggot?
But no, seriously, what was your main argument against mine? You know, I am basically saying having a better life, via a shit wage job, motivates you. That’s all.
I wanna hear what you have to say bro. Or you can keep me jeet all you want either way, you can post whatever you like.

62158791
jeet means victory

62158826
>>62158557
It entirely depends on whether you can keep yourself afloat or not, and I'm not exclusively talking about the financial aspect, being a NEET can take a toll on your mind and if that way of life isn't aligned with your goals then you're gonna suffer more than if you flipped burgers.

62158862
Neet. Obviously. Anyone who says otherwise is coping. lmao.
>t. neet

62158881
>>62158627
Pussy beats being alone.

item.title: BITCOIN CRACKED WITH QUANTUM COMPUTER — 34 replies — 76.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BITCOIN CRACKED WITH QUANTUM COMPUTER

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-04-26 16:55 UTC

OP

Uh oh STINKY..BITCOIN...

Early replies (first 3)

62156686
>>62156684
UHHHH OHHHHH BAGGIES!!!

62156688
>>62156684
NICE TWITTER AD!

62156690
someone needs to build a segregated internet that indians cant join

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156703
>>62156698
BENCHOD

62156732
Algorand bros, we are so fucking back!

62156738
>>62156703
basterd!

62156793
>>62156684
if that were true every single website and bank account would have been hacked by now.

62156864
>>62156684
Short it with 10x leverage then.

62156954
>cryptonobler
one of the most heinous slop accounts, shame on you OP.

>search space of 32,767
Nothingburger as expected.
My phone can crack that in two seconds.

62156982
>>62156684
A QUANTUM COMPOOPER JUST FLEW OVER MY HOUSE

62156988
>>62156684
you don't need a quantum computer for this though, arguably wouldn't even be the best option. a single one of the datacenters used for ai training is more than powerful enough to brute force and validate a gazillion private keys to find a wallet with something in it

62156998
>>62156684
Why would you be invested in an unbacked digital token? You really gotta own tangible appreciating assets, the Schwab tribe will hate you, but so be it.

62157303
lmao
Nic is probably sweating cold right now
everyone is making fun of his project 11 scam
lost essentially ALL is rep in this fraud

62157306
>>62156988
>>62156998
these are bot posts, right? you can't legally be this retarded unless you're some bangladeshi 4'7'' guptacel

62157318
I'm all in on margined gold miners tho

62157322
>>62156732
I'm gonna coooom

62157396
wow he even included a screenshot of some packages being installed... seems legit

62157408
>open Ubuntu
>run apt update
>screenshot it
>post it to fucking 4chan of all places and try to claim that a real, successful quantum attack on Bitcoin just happened
How mentally retarded do you need to be to do this? This doesn't even qualify as bait, it's just fucking stupid.

62157522
>ethbox as hostname and user
noticed that efereum retards were trying to do quantum fud on bitcoin. even got some irrelevant google workers to write a "white paper" on the danger of quantum computing (in like 2040).
here i am hoping quantum actually progresses so that all those old satoshi era coins that havent moved at all can start to circulate again.

62157559
>>62156684
Yet bitcoin isn't crashing hmmmm

62157620
>>62156738
You are the fucking!

62157637
>>62156684
GREAT SOLAR FREAKING FUCKING ROADWAYS SCOTS !

62157651
>>62157620
bastard bitch!

62157659
OP was a tremendous cock sucking brown faggot, as usual.

62157686
>>62157408
Hey Boost is supreme haxxors lib tho

62157775
>>62156732
Based and standing by!

62157799
I fucking bloody piss in your curry benchods for making fun of us pajeets !!!

62158048
HOLY FUCK. I'm out this will kill bitcoin 100%. Fuck qbantum bs that shit should be banned.

62158289
jewgle quantum researcher:
>At the moment, the competition runners seem to be doubling down and trying to defend the utility of the competition. I think that’s a waste of time. The competition failed in the way it was predictably going to fail. Save what credibility you have left and call a duck a duck. Take it on the chin, and be more careful next time.
> https://algassert.com/post/2601
Uh oh, stinky

62158315
>>62156684
I dont listen to news, my news is the price. When the price starts tanking, you know its real. Until then, yawn. Weak fud. Try again

62158325
>15bit keys
You can crack that with pen and paper lmao

62158336
>>62156690
DELTE THS

62158831
>>62156684
>15bit
LMAO not even close

item.title: How do people even come up with this shit ? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do people even come up with this shit ?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-26 17:12 UTC

OP

Some friend recommended me to a "company" he works at, he knew I was looking for extra income

> Yeah bro its fixable hours, fully remote, multinational, basic skills in excel, training included
> oh the title ? Student assistant
> intrigued

Met the "team" on a video call, its like 3-4 people max. Oh man, they are basically running a scam operation where they help rich retards from GULF countries get foreign degrees by basically solving all of their homework, handling EVERYTHING, they fake certificates and whatnot. I ran the number:

>1-2 Million per quarter

I dipped out because I can't handle this shit. How do these fuckers even figure out there is a market for this ? Between the employees and the freelancers they pay, thats around 20 people + 300-500 students paying big buck for the services, and they got people inside these universities to get them our of troubles way .

I wish I had the businesses mind and yeah the friend is indian.

Early replies (first 3)

62158268
i guess just imagine all the things you are proud of, then imagine some loser standing on the sideline thinking "how can i get this without doing the work", then make a product for that loser. especially suppress thought patterns like "if everyone did this society would look like a low trust shithole"

62158279
>>62158259
How is that different from a normal job where you are literally working for a rich retard and solving everything for them?

62158283
>>62158279
Many if not most jobs have nothing to do with working for "rich retards", they're about creating real-world value. My work is in every 777 ever manufactured and helps keep you retards safe when an emergency happens. A different piece of my work helps United Airlines schedule your flights, you see its results every time you use their website to book one.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62158321
>>62158283
Lol
Your in the "my job is important" Era of your life, that's adorable

62158348
Cheating on the SAT is a trillion dollar industry globally. It is just how it is, I dont think degrees are very useful anymore

62158362
>>62158321
if his job wasn't important, you wouldn't have fled into meaningless deflection. you too can become a productive member of society anon

62158372
>>62158283
You make money for your jewish CEO, that's it. Don't delude yourself too much anon.

62158376
>>62158283
Cute post

62158380
>>62158283
Oh look, another code monkey "engineer" who thinks he will still have a job in 5 years. All of you useless slaves deserve what's coming lol.
>>62158362
>you had better start producing, goyim, or else!
>PLEASE?????

62158382
>>62158372
the company makes money because people are willing to pay for their goods and services. you don't have to identify with your job at all, by merely showing up and doing the work you are contributing not only to the wallet of the owners of the company, but to the country at large. capitalism is so effective because it forces the majority of people to contribute. it hurts the conspiracy neet to hear this, but not everything is zero-sum

62158621
>>62158283
your position is absolutely replacable, and when you die everything will keep on going.

62158675
>>62158259
>yeah the friend is indian
Of course he is.
Cheating at university and to get visas is stinkjeet 101.

62158731
>>62158259
>literal nepotism (I don’t know what’s the word for friends)
>anon refuses because “muh principle”
You are a goy cattle destined to live and deal in the real world

62158870
I honestly encourage it, and you should too, the sooner that employers and the general public learn that degrees are fucking worthless shit indicators of ability the faster the job market opens up for everyone.

62158872

62158880

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 93 replies — 68.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-04-26 15:59 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

62143550
Any second now BTC will erupt into a storm that has never been seen before. DOT will soon start it's ascension to the heavens for 75$+. We, the Mumbai warriors, shall eat with silver spoons very soon. The time is coming my warriors. You still have to to buy a big fat bag of DOT before you get priced out.

62143825
>>62129518
>>62131415
>>62131658
>>62131731
>>62131777
>>62135297
>>62139771
>>62143550

This is sad. Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement

62144010
>>62143825
>This is sad.
My brother, the sadness is in your own heart. Not mine. You are destroying yourself.

>Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement
My brother, I am the one and only Vishnu Kumar. Born and raised in India. I am brown as the mud itself. There is no denying it. I am a true Mumbai warrior. I am a proud Indian.
Also Panjali, didn't people teach you that questions are fundamental for a healthy conversation. My young Panjali, I am not here for engagement. If you asked I would've told you so. Oh young Panjali. You still have a long way ahead of you. If accept, I let go, I move on and I forgive you young Panjali.

Thank you for you kind bump of the thread Panjali. I shall later come to your house and give you your share of the profits.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62146419
My fellow Mumbai warriors. We are all already enlightened, we just need to uncover it. DOT is already the best chain, people just need to realize it. They're still looking at the shadow of their light and misidentifying it for themselves. Soon they shall become aware of it and pivot towards the light.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62148896
100$ end of bull run. Remember my Mumbai warriors. It is still cheap as dirt itself. You want to buy now, before you the GIGA ULTRA MEGA pump happens and you get priced out.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62149307
>>62148896
And then have this larping indian short you

62151325
>>62149307
Pssssstttttt Kamal. Do you want to eat with your hands again or do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat like true kings? With spoons? Young warrior Kamal, you have still much to learn.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62153932
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62155237
My brothers... nirvana is near, it's just around the corner. Stack up on DOT right now or get priced out.

62156822
My Mumbai brethren. I've been informed that the Palk Strait will be closed by the Mumbai warriors. We will now toll all ships and they'll have to pay the toll in DOT.

62157778
>>62115424
Im tired

62158070
>>62157778
Good my brother. The first step to enlightenment is to admit and accept that you're tired. You first need to see your darkness in order for you to bring the light. Those who seek the light have experienced immense darkness. Brother, this is a moment of rejoice. Accept your pain, let it go, move on and forgive yourself or the people who have cause it. But remember brother the world is simply an illusion and by that I mean your own perception of reality. It's all inside you and not in the outside world. A river is not beautiful or magnificent, that's your illusion of it. A river is simply water that is moving, nothing more, nothing less. The illusion feels real because just like when you dream the pain feels real, the fear feels real, the sorrow feels real. Once you wake up from the dream you realize that nothing was real, that it was just an illusion. But it is true that when you were in the dream the pain felt real. So, once we see the illusion for what it is it'll start dissolving and once it dissolves that's then the reason to work inside the world without attachments and to help those who are asleep to wake up.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62158170
>>62157778
>>62158070
>The first step to enlightenment is to admit and accept that you're tired.
And then the last step to enlightenment is to realize that there are no steps to enlightenment. It was always there. It was just covered by conditioning, traumas, the ego, ... . Once all of those dissolve it'll uncover enlightenment. So, enlightenment isn't an achievement or something you do, it's literally that you stop doing. It's the fact that the doer stops. The watcher and the watched go away. The only thing that remains is the watching.

62158427
>>
Metoo

62158494
>>62158427
... You forgot to green text young Vishnu mind of the block chain. You too what?

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62158686
If you try to tell a person, "Hey bro, there's hope. You're not a loser. I have faith in you. I think you're a good person.", what do they do? They argue with you. They're defended against that. They intellectualize, "There may be hope for other people, but there's no hope for me.". Such a potent intellectual defense mechanism. And what are they defending against? The belief of hope. Because hope hurts. If there's zero chance, there's zero need for effort. See, human beings are really resilient. We can live through genocide and holocaust and all kinds of stuff. We can live through all kinds of terrible things, terrible economic times. We're survivors. But don't give us hope, because if we can hope, we can hurt.

Having hope also exposes you to hurt. And hurt is a normal part of life. If you're hurting that means you're human. It's just. Are you going to hurt consciously and change something or are you going to hurt unconsciously and keep on hurting due to the same thing for the rest of your life?

You maybe haven't "You go this" at the moment. You may feel powerless. You may feel down. And at the same time you'll slowly and gradually regain your power. You'll unburden the weight. Sooner or later you'll feel so light you'll start flying. I really believe that, even though it may at the current moment not feel like it. Even though you might feel like the current moment is never ending trust me, it'll all change and reshape into a healthier future. You got this fren who's reading this.

Your dear Vishnu Kumar wishes you are peaceful day. May Vishnu of the mindchain be with and all around you.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

item.title: XRP is the biggest scam ever — 39 replies — 46.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP is the biggest scam ever

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-04-26 16:31 UTC

OP

they sell token to shift money over to the tradfi company Ripple to buy actual companies and assets and don't pay any dividends or yield on actual xrp tokens. I've seen actual xrp baggies say this is somehow a good thing.

Early replies (first 3)

62143238
Gee, wonder what they could buy

62143321
>>62143203
Means I can buy more for less. BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62143438
>>62143203
>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what

hypocrites and mind raped baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145068
>>62143560
so ripple buying prime brokerage and countless other institutional leaning companies to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community? crypto tribal fags are so pathetic

62146230
>>62143438
>>62144998
>>62145068
Ripple dumps on TOKEN HOLDERS to fund things that DO NOT USE THE TOKEN, and explicitly only benefit SHAREHOLDERS.

Nobody else in crypto does this.
How are you people so insanely retarded.

62146371
>>62146230
Chainlink does the exact same thing you dense paid mouthbreather shill
None of the shit that CLL promotes or builds or advertizes has anything to do whit the token itself

62146402
>>62146371
If it uses CL nodes, it uses the token.
So to prove your position, all you have to do is name one CLL product that does not use the nodes.
Go

62146438
>>62146402
name one instance when you had to purchase the token
go

62146458
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

woosh go the goalposts

62146481
>>62146458
>muh subsidies mean token needed despite the fact that absolutely no one buys it or uses it for anything
you're unironically a moron if you truly believe semantics and nuances are doing anything for you
you've been trying this angle for 6 years to no avail, read the room you spastic

62146485
>>62146371
>it has nothing to do with the token

>>62146438
>ok it does need the token, but you didn't purchase it directly

>>62146481
>ok purchasing directly or indirectly is irrelevant, but some of it is subsidized

Second goalpost move in a row.

62146502
>>62146485
not a single product requires the usage of LINK
no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
not a single trial or pilot requires the LINK token at any capacity, not a single institutional partner has ever touched the token
can't wait until you'll finally get doxed and raped in Minecraft

62146506
>>62146502
>not a single product requires the usage of LINK
All products use CL nodes, therefore requiring the usage of Link.

You're probably going to loop right back to "but you didn't purchase the Link directly".

>no, subsidies do not matter at all, sorry
But you're the one who brought up the subsidies.

What a shitty chatbot.

62146532
>>62146506
you do realize that once Chainlink implodes, you along with all the other paid marketeers will be in grave danger? you do realize that attacking your entire online persona around a crypto scamcoin will inevitably end bad for you?
tick, tock...

62146544
>>62146532
>paid marketeer
I'm not the one lying, moving goalposts, forgetting my own talking points, and changing IDs.

Go ahead, name one CL product that does not use CL nodes (and therefore the Link token).

62146565
>>62146506
kill yourself before I find you, Rajesh

62146578
>says some bullshit
>gets debunked
>changes IDs and gets extremely emotional

many such cases, sad!

62147333
>>62145068
>to create their ecosystem is just pure dumping on the community?
What ecosystem? The one they claim is coming? None of the companies they bought have integrated XRPL.

62147341
>>62143203
>dont come to /biz/ for a year
>its still the same fucking threads by cultist linkies and xrp schizos
We’ll see you next year

62147491
>>62147341
it's literally the same 4 retards in every fucking thread

62147758
>>62146458
literally the same can be said of xrp kek

62147964
Chainlink is such a pathetic little project...

62148311
>>62147341
next year chainlink will be dead and buried though, and the catalogue will be flooded with frogposters crying about "why didn't you tell me????"

62151224
>>62147758
Fucking how?

62154431
>>62146230
Ripple don't sell to retail and their products are building around actual use. Chainlink sell every green candle directly to retail and built zero utility for the token that is itself completely disconnected from the actual product.

62155143
>>62154431
>Ripple don't sell to retail
After the lawsuit and the SEC waiver, if Ripple wanted to sell to institutions they'd have to file an application with the SEC saying "we want to sell securities".

These applications are public, so anyone can verify that Ripple have never filed such an application to sell XRP to institutions: https://www.sec.gov/edgar/browse/?CIK=1685012
Thus they've been selling to retail exclusively.

>built zero utility for the token
See >>62146402
Link token utility is absolute; every single CL feature and product requires it.

Meanwhile Ripple pretty much only builds products and buys companies that have nothing inherently to do with XRP.

62155954
i made this thread
you are all spiritually indian
when indians get criticized they say “well pakistan is shittier!”. i dont care about chainlink, i dont invest in link, its probably a paypig investor scam too, i made this thread about xrp and you xrp schizos all had to create this charicture in ur mind about how i must be a link shill

no
of ur first thought after reading a criticism of xrp is “well at least we arent chainlink investors” then,
you are spiritually indian

62156263
>>62155143
None of you have ever been able to explain how that would cause the price of the fundraising token to increase. If node operators are paid in lank, then they immediately turn around and sell those tokens for actual money, doesn’t that just increase sell pressure? I’m also slightly retarded, so feel free to explain it to me

62156366
>>62156263
>If node operators are paid in lank, then they immediately turn around and sell those tokens for actual money, doesn’t that just increase sell pressure?
That's how blockchain miners have always operated, you dip.

62156399
>>62156366
oh so it's not the future

62156865
>>62156399
Blockchain is absolutely the future. And it needs external data to become it.

62158109
>>62143238
Land

62158769
>>62147333
Checked but a 20 second search tells me you're incorrect.

item.title: >tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this? — 56 replies — 34.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this?

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-04-26 15:43 UTC

OP

>tfw fell for the NEET meme
how the fuck can you guys endure this? it's so boring and depressing, I unironically miss my wagecuck 9-5

Early replies (first 3)

62156768
I would unironically wagecuck still if I was able to take vacation whenever I want. I don't enjoyed scheduling things like that or requiring permission.

62156775
>>62156751
I became a neet because of crippling social anxiety, not out of choice.
I suspect many are the same.

62156779
>>62156751
see you fell for it without having any money, I did that once and then realized that it costs money to do anything other than sit in front of a screen all day every day

Depending on where you live, even going out for a walk will cost you bus fare or gas to get to a decent park. Never felt so trapped, now if I had money to go out and do things like camping or fishing or even just cook nice meals then I would have been a lot happier

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62157126
>>62156779
Who the fuck is too poor to afford a bus what is this larp. If you can't ride a bus how do you have a roof over your head

62157134
>>62156917
>if you eat great food every day, eventually you'll be so bored you'll start eating shit
>if you sit on a comfy chair every day, eventually you'll be so bored you'll start sitting with a knife up your ass
I don't think that's how boredom works

62157204
>>62157117
I hear you. I don’t have the time nor the energy like I did back then; waging just drains a man for a pittance.
>>62156917
I fucked up so many times. It would be nice to wake up and it all fades away.

62157215
>>62157134
lmao!
Thats pretty much what wagies say
I just told some guy
>i am 35 and I never had a job
You know what he said with a sneer on his face?
>thats not the flex you think it is
LOL

62157256
>>62156905
>>62156794
how do you guys feel about AI

62157293
>>62157256
I WISH I HAD HELD ON TO MY MICRON LETF AT 50 BUCKS IN DECEMBER

NOW THAT IT'S 250 BUCKS

62157298
>>62157134
>>62157215
to be fair if you gave me a million bucks/financial security I would 'wagie' by trying to become a corporate finance lawyer, because I would want to have 10 million and 100 million and be a CEO. Because I can't stand being a broke bitch. Give me 70 years so I can become charlie munger or warren buffet. Fuck this poorfag shit.

62157475
>>62157298
None of the two people you mentioned came from rags, bro. Lol.

62157483
>>62157475
Yes and Trump got a million dollar loan

But even if he lost the loan his dad was worth half a billion anyway so he had no real risk. My point is if you give me financial security so that I don't feel like putting 100k into the market could bankrupt me or make me, I would be so much more than I am. But the system actively tries to fuck you in the ass.

Like playing as Wallachia in Victoria 3, you spend the entire game trying to absorb Moldova and liberate yourself and get great powers to help you fuck up Austria to get your wider territory back. By the time you're a great power it's already 1900 and the game is almost over.

62157525
>>62157483
2.5% of Americans have 1m

62157542
>>62157126
Most NEETs live really tight. They act smug about it here but they need to budget everything. I never fully could go NEETing because here and there I always did some tutoring to have some money for shit beyond food. I also missed out all the possible moonshots from 2012 onwards, including triple digit BTC and double digit ETH, single digit AMD, etc. because I never had money. In hindsight I could have skipped a computer upgrade back in 2019 but still it wouldn't made me a millionaire.
Dating also becomes impossible after 27. Girls start asking what you do for a living, and answering "I'm a college student" and "I live with my parents" is no longer good for them.
>>62156871
What hobbies can someone have when living with parents that set the rules in a small house? Painting? Writing? Cycling?

62157561
>>62157542
> and "I live with my parents" is no longer good for them.
If they know you don’t come from a rich family, yeah.

62157574
>>62157542
>Most NEETs live really tight. They act smug about it here but they need to budget everything
Who doesn’t have a budget? Rich and poors have a budget. Hell, it is probably only the rich that even have a budget.

62157589
>>62157525
>>62157542
AMD will probably run higher

But in just the last 8 months if you bought tech shit like Sandisk you could have made it, if you bought with 100k.

62157602
It's a quiet life. Being a neet in your teens/20s is fucked up, you're never gonna look better or have more energy.

But after that, if you didn't make it, why not? If you have the means to live without working. Dealing with bosses, with coworkers, traffic... sorry but nope.

62157704
>>62157542
>Girls start asking what you do for a living
i work in finance, managing a private pool of equity, with a focus on bank-to-bank transfer agents that facilitate trillions a year aka pools closed

62157756
>>62156751
>how the fuck can you guys endure this
They don't, they hate themselves.

62157769
>been neet since 2020
The biggest thing I have faced is what do you do when you have nothing to cope with? Most people's lives involve working and doing something to blow off steam after work. So it becomes a stress/release cycle. The boredom that comes is from doing all the release without the stress part. eventually your brain gets bored, and you don't really know who you are outside of that cycle.

Other consequences are that you don't realize how much structure a job really provides you. Sleep schedule, social outlets, and sense of belonging, to name a few. You really have to have your shit together because once that structure is gone, you're only left with yourself.

Some pro's are that you can really slow down to learn yourself. Take a full week trying something to see if it's for you. I've gone to college just to be a frat boy, went to a lineman school to see blue collar life, and worked in a bank to see white collar. More experiences than most, and my idea of the ideal life for me has formed more out of these experiences, without the pressure of needing to succeed in those fields. Which strangely makes me perform better "detachment" wink wink.

>become a master of finding and routing out your negative self-defeating talk
This is another pro, you get a lot of time to work on, and I believe Jesus when he said the kingdom of heaven is within you. When you can sit quietly with your mind, you can view your patterns clearly over a long period of time, and with the help of a therapist, really root out the major mental blocks.

You need to be selfish and learn yourself (slay your dragon) before you start your real life. Many people I see are so eager to pursue their career, family, and goals without knowing why they even want them.

62157987
>>62156751
Did this, regretted it. My advice is if you're gonna beer then have a social life, healthy diet and ample amounts of sunlight. Also learn something. If you don't want to be a wagie then ok but isolating yourself leads to mental and psychological issues.

62158000
>>62157215
I met a dude who was past 50 and he never had a job before. I asked him for advice lol.

62158001
>>62156751
just get fat, unironically
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jtwIERgy2OE

62158010
>>62156838
The majority of people is born to be cattle. They do not possess any internal motivation.

62158383
>>62157756
Having a job doesn't make things better tho. I'm still a pessimist Chud, except with less time for myself and a bit more money that I don't really use

62158410
>>62158001
maybe if you want to die, all those body positivity activists are dead now, some in their early 20s too

62158441
>could NEET due to zog disability payments
>choose not to to upstage my dad in net worth
>odds stacked against me due to boomer/zoomer financial divide
Sampler fry

62158455
>>62157602
never gonna have more anxiety or be more unsure of myself either. i realised that what i had to do was accept who i am and im not just not very good at the life thing.

62158467
i was a neet for 10 (ten) years no im working in a corporate office like a pajeet from the helpdesk but still

62158571
>>62156751
I haven't worked since 2015, but sometimes I have dreams where I decide to show up at my old work and just go to my desk and start working like nothing happened. During the dream I go to work for a year, working for free before I decide to ask my boss to start paying me, including back pay. He says "no" and starts laughing and I wake up in a panic.
What a nightmare that is, i hate that recurring dream.

62158622
you have to do something, you have to have an activity and goals. It's easy for me to say because I went to school for CS so my hobby is programming and so I can just spend all day in front of a PC and feel fulfilled, but if you're not into that you need to find some activity you can do that won't bankrupt you and provide you with meaning.

The germans were right, work will set you free, but people conflate wagecucking with work. Being a wagecuck will not set you free

62158636
>>62156751
I had to persuade myself that not having a job was worse than looking for a job.

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 65 replies — 11.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-04-26 11:32 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62148947
>>62141874
What makes you think that?

62149370
>>62146661
Like >>62147680 it's was around $1000

62149399
>>62142226
Did you enjoy losing 3 mil, fucker?

62149941
>>62138453
Only my brother knows I bought BTC, we both stake together now as well, with native staking gaining traction. Imagine if I told my parents to buy at 120K Kek.

62150719
>>62142197
You never know

62150903
>>62148933
Still it has risks

62151073
>>62146671
I get that, but it’s better to check first before saying that. So far, it fits my standards. I don’t think firms like Ledger or even A16z would back it otherwise.

62151201
>>62148936
Greedy bastard

62151260
>>62151073
it's the same thing. Everything will fail in the end. Nothing survive

62151310
NEVER

62151368
>>62149941
Heartaches

62151461
>>62138453
How do we confess them?

62151818
>>62149941
Nice are you also waiting for the TBV launch? More interesting for me since it could unlock more use cases.

62152719
>>62142226
That's the goal lol

62152732
never ever tell anyone you own any crypto holy shit that is like rule #1 in life don't tell me you idiots are trying to orange pill your fucking friends and family

62152763
>>62138453
Why would you ever brag about your money? Especially when every cryptobro is a scrawny geek. Do you want to get tortured by niggers for your private key?

Secret wealthy is better

62153574
>>62150903
Every system has risk. The difference is just what kind of risk you’re exposed to and how much control you still keep over your assets.

62153589
>>62152732
Maybe it depends on who you’re closest to in your family. There’s usually one person you really vibe with.

62153929
>>62152763
Do what you wanna do

62154078
>>62142464
This here is the right play. Don't tell anyone how much money you have or flaunt it. It should be used to give you freedom not ego validation. Also when you reveal wealth to people they will try to use you and get some for themselves. Donate to charity and volunteer also though of course.

62154092
If you talk about bitcoin, people either assume you’re a sucker, a drug dealer, evading taxation, or a pedophile. I think what really limits its reach is that it’s technology, economics and political philosophy wrapped into an asset. Never selling.

62155160
>>62142464
Well...she's still your gf so do that...

62155300
>>62154092
Think the opposite

62155673
>>62155300
What’s the opposite?

62155774
I haven't told anyone how much I have invested, I just tell them my various investment schemes so they hopefully do something.
It's especially important for the kids because their zog parents tell them not to invest and to get a car for MUH FREEDOMS.

62155914
>>62151310
Ok, that's not a problem

62156005
Guys check out my crypto currency called hsgold I’m going to add a fair bit of liquidity at the end of the month when I get paid check it out highseasgold currently swapable for sol

62156014
>>62138453
That's RULE NUMBER ONE of the Ten Crack Commandments anon.

62157978
>>62138453
Don't do it

62158303
>>62154078
You are right too. Let's see anon.

item.title: >be me >have decent paying job at warehouse >hate it with all my being and turned me into an alcoholic >finally decided… — 14 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>be me >have decent paying job at warehouse >hate it with all my being and turned me into an alcoholic >finally decided…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-26 15:45 UTC

OP

>be me
>have decent paying job at warehouse
>hate it with all my being and turned me into an alcoholic
>finally decided to quit
>be me
>unemployed NEET
>bored of everything and feel my sanity slipping away everyday

What the actual fuck are you supposed to even do in life? Everything just feels like one massive scam.

Early replies (first 3)

62158392
Goyim in the real world.

62158393
you are a slave, you failed the freedom test

go back to your cage or kys

stupid dalit

62158396
>>62158385
You're supposed to neet when you can afford to never work again aka retire

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62158397
>>62158385
I experienced the same massive boredom and lack of stuff to do for half a year. Then I started playing oldschool runescape and it gave me something to do every day. (Its a game people often play over 10k hours)

62158400
>>62158385
>can't entertain himself with the vast world outside, his own interests and hobbies, or even playing games
>only feels satisfied when prodooooooocing for his overlords
ngmi. you are essentially a golem at this point anon. the only thing that could help is trying to retrain your brain away from the jewish reward signaling scheme that you have been exploited by for years.

62158417
>>62158385
Become a security guard anon. I used to be an order selector/forklift driver/machine operator and it all blows ass.

I’ve been in security for 3 years now and I just sit on my ass on a $1200 herman miller Aeron chair in a luxury office building overnight doing nothing. I’ve worked for two companies and I can just sit on my ass in comfy chairs the entire shift. Barely have to do anything or talk to anyone.

I might do this for the rest of my life. It’s too comfy.

62158475
>>62158385
pretty normal

62158479
>>62158385
get a hobby
if your an alcoholic, start brewing beer or something
if literally all you have is your warehouse skills, start a hobby warehouse i guess

62158495
>>62158400
Living only for your own hedonism is a pathetic, degenerate state of being. Funny how you ”right wingers” always forget that.

62158536
you thought you wanted to play vidya and watch animu all day long, but these were not your desires. you just read people talking about how good it is on the board and accepted that viewpoint as your own, which it never was. you can now begin your journey of self-disovery

62158546
>>62158536
When I was a neet I was afraid of dying because my life was so fun and I didn't want the fun to end.
Guess how I feel now being a wagie

62158552
>>62158385
If I had no prospects in life I'd get my CDL and become a truck driver. Warehouse work sucks after college or you early 20s

62158629
>>62158385
I mean you had an income, why didn’t you spend time doing what you like then?
Go out, get a good pc and play GREAT games , spend money on good products that makes you look and feel good.
I get it 100% how a job can sucks, but I don’t get how can’t you compensate that suckiness with other shit?
>>62158393
Anon he failed when he had a job.
People like this unironically become their jobs and then when they lose it, they also lose themselves
Job to me, is just a way to earn; it has literal zero consequences on my life

62158640
>>62158392
employim

item.title: Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it). — 53 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it).

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-04-26 09:14 UTC

OP

Why the FUCK is life so fucking difficult (particularly the financial aspect of it).

Early replies (first 3)

62155115
>>62155108
it's the way capitalism works, it's an annihilationist force that is counter to life. it will continue until we're all dead and earth has been completely liquidated. I dont' know why we do this to ourselves

>uncleted.jpg

62155117
LUCK is the biggest factor in life.

62155122
>>62155115
Just like cancer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155588
>>62155530
Stiffle competition.

62155599
The ages don't line up with what you biologically want to do
>As a kid / teenager
This is where you have the most TVM to benefit you, but you can't have a job and if you got one you're just buying a gaming computer and working part-time
>20's
This is when you have good TVM to benefit from but you're just starting in whatever field, or possibly still in school. A natural human doesn't feel like planning for 40 years in the future here, he just wants to make babies and do cool things.
>Late 20's approaching 30's
This is where you feel like you have to start a business or build something or get married - this happens universally to everyone who still has their natural human instincts. At some point you just see what you needed to do in the past to be successful today, and now it's up to yourself from the past to have the capital to make whatever you want to do now work, but they don't have the same motives as you.
>>62155173
Early 20's, not going all the way to 30's with this.
If you spent like 5 years moneymaxxing and just investing as much as possible that would pretty much set you up for life even in a relatively low paying job, even with a mcjob if you just live with your parents and buy rental properties.
>>62155198
>you could literally live with your parents, collect welfare and then go all in on stocks while eating chicken tendies. how is it difficult? you're lucky you weren't born during the middle ages
Yup, more kids need to be told this. Live with your parents, work 5 years investing everything, you've just saved 20 or 30 years of work later.
It's really simple but instead we have these people told they need to go 100,000 in debt and blow 4-6 years of their life studying, when instead they could make 100,000 in that time frame that's all invested.

62155603
>>62155108
I made 7 million dollars slinging shitcoins and I have a tested IQ of 104. Might be a skill issue.

62155607
>>62155603
You can't even retire on 7 million these days. That's at best 5 years of retirement with today's inflation.

62155612
You feel difficult brcauze you don’t have. Little sister like me who can let me breeding press her everyday when my bag is full.

62155614
>>62155607
You can pseudo-retire on 100k if you live with parents and take up a couple hundred in odd jobs per month. Not a true retirement, more like a "work a day a week" thing.

62155675
>>62155108
in the past (pre 2020) it used to be cause people didnt know how to invest and bought shit they didnt need. now its wealth inequality, they're literally siphoning all producitivity gains

62155705
I find it easy to save and invest, but it’s extraordinarily difficult to find an opportunity, go all-in with size, and do a 10x. In this life, you either accept wagecuckery until 62-65, you hypergamble in the market, or you become an entrepreneur.

62155765
>>62155705
Yeah, 10x's are hard but 2x's just happen consistently, so it's more about doing it when you're younger.
A little bit of wagecuckery chasing 2x's goes a long way when you're 20 and those will double like 4 times before retirement.

62155773
>>62155765
The issue is you see an asset that is going parabolic, you say “yeah, this is it” and then it does 2x, 4x, etc. but the very fucking moment you hop in, you’re down 30 percent. Right now, everyone is getting rich on the AI bottleneck trade. If I wasn’t holding bitcoin, I’d just say fuck it and hop in.

62155777
>>62155198
>you could literally live with your parents, collect welfare and then go all in on stocks while eating chicken tendies. how is it difficult? you're lucky you weren't born during the middle ages
lmao literally me except i dont eat tendies
i am currently investing 100% in ftse all world and while it grows well idk if i can escape the neet life with it. also i can also only save up to 10k before the state cuts my neet money
dont even know what i would do with the money if i got lucky

62155782
>>62155773
I've been holding more cash specifically because of the AI stuff, I still invest but I think some kind of downturn is coming and I want to be able to jump at opportunities.
I really would never go all-in on some kind of "ai bottleneck", it's just gambling. Sure, gamble with 25% if you really want to.

Recently houses have been going for 100k in my area (lockport new york because people will say this is fake) and I'm waiting for an even further dip as boomers die off, which has actually been happening because all land owners in my area are 75.

62155861
>>62155782
The outsized returns come with the gambling. Don’t get me wrong, all of my money is in VOO and bitcoin. But it causes deep distress to see people printing off of leveraged sector ETFs. Real estate is sometimes the move. Homes that were $100k in 2002 are now sometimes $1.3m. But I’ve also seen other areas that were the next big thing, and they’ve crabbed for decades.

My take? New York has had net population losses for a while. Buffalo is doing better in recent years, but will it extend to Lockport? Unsure.

62155878
>>62155115
>>62155122
>>62155117
Can't believe you retards haven't figured out the real answer. I'll give you a hint, it's jews.

62155879
>>62155861
>But will it extend to Lockport?
It already has, but in rural areas. My city is in a decline in population and nobody's making any money here and never will, which is where my edge comes in.
Since I'm not 75 and not literally dying to sell off my shit to pay for a new truck I can get along maintaining and renting houses, and it's no big loss to have one I have to sell because I can do basically all cosmetic work myself.

But people are desperate to escape city shitholes so small suburb areas and even lockport have seen buffalo growth.

62155881
>>62155108
Because of genetic determinism and how the modern world works. We have abundance thanks to modern tech and slave labor from third world countries but everyone wants to have access to the wealth. Meanwhile the state simply prints more money making your money worth less. The only way to counter this is by having assets that are not bound to this or scale higher then inflation. We still have law of nature, the fittest aka the most adaptable wins and will be on top, just that most people who would die normally are being dragged on for some reason.

62155923
>>62155108
just stop caring
i havent done laundry in over a year. my apartment is a disaster and i eat like once every 3-4 days out of pure lazyness.
i have no friends never go out other than work.
i take several stimulants to keep my brain on autopilot at work so im not really "there"
i recently lost the will to commute so i just uber it both ways
when i get home i just rot in bed, look at the numbers move and watch oneyplays until i pass out.
sometimes ill play something on my 'eck if im feeling especially motivated.
i just dont care anymore.

62155978
>>62155108
too many people

62156145
>>62155259
>you are framed and jailed for sexual assault,
kek, definitely a thing that happens to normal, well-adjusted people

62156342
>>62155878
jews are huma....oh wait!

62156367
>>62155198
What to invest in tho?
Nothing gives more than 5% annual returns these days unless you do degenerate options gambling

62156382
>>62155130
>where it was financially optimal to take care of your cows and pigs so that they lived happy lives
This is impossible regardless of how well you treat your livestock. Beef cattle are typically slaughtered when they're somewhere between 1-2 years old. Dairy cattle are slaughtered once their milk production starts to decline, around 5 years old.

The natural lifespan of a cow is 20-30 years. You could treat your cattle incredibly well but you're still killing them when they're basically children. Killing a 1-2 year old cow is equivalent to killing a 6-8 year old human. If you were treated very well for the first 8 years of your life before being slaughtered so some alien species could eat you, would you consider that you lived a happy life?

62156385
>>62155108
in order for the few percentage of rich population to enjoy the leisure time, you need a society of people jsut barely getting by so they need to be at work as much as possible making either you or your rich buddies money. all the while keeping you out of their way for a majority of the week.

62156388
>>62156382
I'd have been eaten in 2000, being a wagecuck in 2026.

62156412
>>62155159
seriously though I have this, idk, risk aversion disability where even if something is 99% likely to make big gains if I just hold for a year, I will constantly panic sell and then cry afterwards. I don't know what the fuck is wrong with me. It's happened like 5 times now.

62156445
>>62155923
>oneyplays

dude go get some help tf

62156449
>>62156367
you have to gamble if you want to win. There are too many people on earth for you to make it without taking on risk.

62156515
>>62156476
pretty much nailed it

62156850
>>62155878
Stop noticing

62158142
>>62156388
The correct way

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 56 replies — 5.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:26 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

62147432
Izzat farming thread
Abandon ship

62147439
It costs zero dollars to not be so darned rude

62147451
>>62143054

fuck i forgot about pomodoro. imagine having a faggy little tomato timer on your desk as an adult

62148909
>>62144289
this

62151619
>>62144395
... if you want to end up in hell

62151984
>>62144435
Marcus Aurelius didn't trust the slut, he knew she was bad when he married her. Thing was, she was the daughter of the previous emperor who was a cool guy and Aurelius married her out of loyalty to him (plus he got to be emperor himself out of it).

62152046
>>62143054
for me its several different stimulants and an ssri
amazingly im still a husk of a human, its honestly impressive financially speaking

62152662
Scam

62152741
>>62143054
Many of those things have improved my life a lot. Of course, I am not a billionaire but I like to enjoy the little things in life

62152905
>>62145005
You're supposed to read good fiction and let your mind wander, not that (((self help))) slop.

62155066
>>62143054
tbqh deepest post on this board

62155320
>>62147439
opportunity cost of missing a zinger tho

62156116
stupid demoralizing thread

62156188
>>62152741
>Many of those things have improved my life a lot
like what

62156224
>>62144272
>>62144395
>t.

62156315
>>62143054
My coming has gotten out of control that I don't even try to do nofap anymore. From 16-21 I was extremely passionate about but now at 27 I unironically have multiple screens of degeneracy on play and I even plan out my sessions.

62156324
>>62143054
Ask yourself this. Do any richfag elitists like Bezos do any of this?

62156689
>>62143054
This made me laugh so hard idk. I cant believe take things so seriously, it's sad

62157981
>>62156188
Improved my sperm count

62156636

62157781

62158577

item.title: Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p… — 47 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:15 UTC

OP

Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the previous “Buy One, Add One for $1” menu AKA the only reason to have the app anymore after the deals turned to shit. I’ve also noticed with this update the drink prices increased by $0.20, a $0.40 increase YoY in my area. Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger. Went home and ate some eggs and toast.

Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62155716
>>62155708
Give it up, brother. Your fat ass doesn't need to be eating McDick's anyway.

62155723
>>62155708
For me, it's Wendy's. They still have a decent meal deal although it has gone up $1 over the past couple years. Ironically they have been a better value than Micky d's for a little while now.
>>62155716
Sounds like projection tb.h

62155728
>>62155708
learn to cook

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155851
>>62155748
To be fair, that's canadian dollars which are worth much less than usd

62155867
>>62155851
Canada and Australia should adopt a different dollar sign. They casually post prices in their shitty cheap plastic "dollars" on the internet like it's USD, and that confuses everyone.

62155874
>>62155708
>Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger.
the goyim have gone insane

62155922
Anyone still eating McDonald’s can go get McFucked. I remember eating there as a kid in the 80s and they used to have events where some pedo used to larp as Ronald McDonald. The playground and food was great too. It was wholesome. The jews ruined that, along with everything else. Pizza Hut was also top tier. Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.

62155943
>>62155708
"Product"

62155947
>>62155922

62155976
>>62155922
>Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.
while the quality of ingredients at least outside the US has improved massively over the years, the overall price-to-value ratio has reached a point where you can get proper food at a restaurant for sometimes less but more often than not the same amount of money.
it just doesn't make sense anymore and too much of it is still bad for you so I guess I agree with you there.

62156013
McD is like the only cheap fast food place at this point.

$7.59 for a large meal. I just checked Wendys for a similar meal and they charge $11.59!

62156034
Will ozempic taken by every fatass in the country make mcdonalds drop their prices or will the influx of thinner people that think they can suddenly eat more make them raise?

62156037
>>62155708
It was $2.12 using the apps 40% off deal to get my one double cheeseburger because I didn't want two for $4, now it's $1.70. This works out better for me as long as they don't remove the app discount.

62156039
>>62155708
ill have 2 large fries no salt
4 mcdoubles extra onion extra ketchup add mayo
4 hot and spicy mcchickens extda mayo extra lettuce
40 mcnuggets all habanero ranch
big mac
uhhhhhhhhhhhh

62156052
>>62155737
>Top post on the FIRE subreddit is complaining about how much the price of taco bell has increased
If Taco Bell impacts your finances that much then you don't have enough to retire

62156090
>he didn’t take profits on the $5 meal deal
ngmi

62156160
>>62155732
Ok but why visit a real restaurant or at least some small business?

62156273
>>62155708
That fucking mcchicken used to be $1. Why is mcniggers still trying to call this shit a “value” menu.

62156289
>>62156273
yeah in 1980 dude. holy fuck for a finance subreddit this place sure does suck sometimes.
picrel is the menu around 1975.

62156291
>>62156273
honestly it should still be a buck, no way that sandwich costs more than 50 cents to make

62156292
>>62156289
the mcchicken was a dollar in the 2010's

62156294
>>62156292
sure, and rent was $500 and employees only made $4 per hour and insurance rates were cheap and franchising costs were cheap.

62156301
>>62156294
Why are you mad and wrong?
>>62156292
This poster is factually accurate. McD is crazy expensive now.

62156370
>>62155708
>crying that his goysyrup went up 20¢

62156472
>>62156160
Because I cannot be in two places at the same time. At least not in my corporeal form.

62156693
>>62155708
What happened to the DOLLAR MENU

Not the buy one and get one for a dollar I mean

THE DOLLAR MENU

62156721
>>62155716
I mean really. If the price of McDonald's is so high it bothers you then you're eating too much McDonald's, no way around it.

62156722
>>62155737
There has been a resurgence in smug girl posts with quality rebuttals

62156723
>>62155976
At this point it's a mouse trap

62156726
>>62155708
Fast food doesn't understand people eat their slop solely because its cheap. Probably for the better they price themselves out since people don't need to be eating that garbage anyways

62156731
These $5 meals are pathetic because I would need at least 2 of them to feel satisfied.

62156748
>>62155708
nothing on that menu should be over 99¢, and half of it should be less than 75¢

62157963
>>62155708
Those product looks gross
